subject: PATIENT
heading: Brief Hx & Hospital Course:__page_no__7
text: brief hx & hospital course: 83-year-old female who was admitted for chest pain and abdominal pain and right knee pain. cardiology cleared the patient since her troponin was elevated but it was recommended it was probably a type ii nstemi due to acute enteritis and diverticulitis. ct scan of the abdomen showed diverticulosis with acute enteritis and diverticulitis and therefore she was treated conservatively with a clear liquid diet and iv antibiotics. gradually her symptoms improved. yesterday her diet was advanced and today she is doing better. she will be discharged home now she will be discharged home on cipro and flagyl for 7 days. she is also requesting norco which she takes at home for pain. she is ambulating independently and therefore she can be discharged home today and follow-up with her primary care physician in 1 to 2 weeks and resume her home medications.

text: brief hx & hospital course: 83-year-old female who was admitted for chest pain and abdominal pain and right knee pain. cardiology cleared the patient since her troponin was elevated but it was recommended it was probably a type ii nstemi due to acute enteritis and diverticulitis. ct scan of the abdomen showed diverticulosis with acute enteritis and diverticulitis and therefore she was treated conservatively with a clear liquid diet and iv antibiotics. gradually her symptoms improved. yesterday her diet was advanced and today she is doing better. she will be discharged home now she will be discharged home on cipro and flagyl for 7 days. she is also requesting norco which she takes at home for pain. she is ambulating independently and therefore she can be discharged home today and follow-up with her primary care physician in 1 to 2 weeks and resume her home medications.

text: exam demonstrate wall thickening and mild adjacent stranding, may be seen with infectious or inflammatory enteritis in the appropriate clinical setting. appendix is not visualized. extensive diverticulosis involving the rectosigmoid colon and, to a lesser extent the rest of the colon. wall thickening of the rectosigmoid colon, likely sequelae of chronic diverticular disease. there is some stranding in the lower abdomen, which appears to be centered near adjacent small bowel loops, but also appears to extend adjacent to the sigmoid colon. favor adjacent inflammation associated with the process involving the small bowel, although secondary inflammation of the sigmoid colon not excluded. small amount of free fluid in the lower abdomen and pelvis, likely inflammatory in nature. pelvic organs: the uterus is anteverted. numerous phleboliths are seen in the pelvis. bladder: unremarkable. no mass. abdominal wall: no mass or hernia. bones: severe osteoarthritic changes are seen in both hips, right greater than left with joint space narrowing, subchondral sclerosis, and subchondral cystic change, as well as prominent marginal osteophytes. there is levoconvex curvature of the lumbar spine and multilevel severe disc space narrowing with associated endplate sclerosis and endplate spurring. no evidence of acute fracture. no suspicious intraosseous lesion visualized.
